---
title: "Untitled"
author: "T.Laffargue"
date: "2025-02-10"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```
```{r}
setwd("~/Desktop/thèse/Microbiome Biodiversity/Bacteria and fungi/Typing Drosophila/All sequence")

############################################ install packages #########################################################



if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))
    install.packages("BiocManager")
BiocManager::install("Biostrings")

install.packages("dplyr")

install.packages("rentrez")
install.packages("httr")
install.packages("xml2")
install.packages("stringr")
install.packages("readxl")
install.packages("writexl")

########################################## Loading libraries ###################################################

library(httr)
library(rentrez)
library(Biostrings)
library(xml2)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(readxl)
library(tidyr)
library(writexl)

#############################################################################################################
##################################### clean the Blast_result_table ##########################################
#############################################################################################################

#### Checking results ####
Blast_result_brute <- readr::read_csv("blast_results.csv") #results importation
View(Blast_result_brute) #Visualization 

Number_of_samples <- unique(Blast_result_brute$qseqid) #Verify that sample are well blasted
Number_of_samples

#### Clean columns ####

Blast_result_brute$Species <- sub("^.*?((Drosophila|Scaptomyza|Scaptodrosophila|Zaprionus)\\s+\\w+).*", "\\1", Blast_result_brute$stitle)# keep only species names

colnames(Blast_result_brute)[colnames(Blast_result_brute) == "qseqid"] <- "Sample_ID" #rename col
colnames(Blast_result_brute)[colnames(Blast_result_brute) == "sseqid"] <- "HitID"
colnames(Blast_result_brute)[colnames(Blast_result_brute) == "pident"] <- "Percentage_Identity"
colnames(Blast_result_brute)[colnames(Blast_result_brute) == "qcovs"] <- "Query_Cover"

Blast_result_brute <- Blast_result_brute[, !(names(Blast_result_brute) %in% c(
  "length", "mismatch", "gapopen", "qstart", "qend", "sstart", "send", 
  "evalue", "bitscore", "qcovhsp", "stitle"
))]
View(Blast_result_brute)

write_xlsx(Blast_result_brute, "Blast_result_brute.xlsx")

#### filter results ####

# create list to store intermediate results (species/samples)
filtered_results_species <- list()

# unique value treatment
for (sample in unique(Blast_result_brute$Sample_ID)) {
  for (species in unique(Blast_result_brute$Species)) {
    
    # select line corresponding to species and sample
    subset_data <- Blast_result_brute[Blast_result_brute$Sample_ID == sample & Blast_result_brute$Species == species, ]
    
    # verify if such line exist
    if (nrow(subset_data) > 0) {
      
      # Verify if at least one line has query cover > 90
      if (any(subset_data$Query_Cover > 90)) {
        subset_data <- subset_data[subset_data$Query_Cover > 90, ]  # keep only query cover > 90
      }
      
      # find max Percentage_Identity
      max_identity <- max(subset_data$Percentage_Identity, na.rm = TRUE)
      subset_data <- subset_data[subset_data$Percentage_Identity == max_identity, ]  # keep lines with max
      
      # if more than one line, keep highest query_cover
      if (nrow(subset_data) > 1) {
        max_cover <- max(subset_data$Query_Cover, na.rm = TRUE)
        subset_data <- subset_data[subset_data$Query_Cover == max_cover, ]  # keep highest query_cover
      }
      
      # If more than one line stays keep only the first
      if (nrow(subset_data) > 1) {
        subset_data <- subset_data[1, , drop = FALSE]  # keep the first
      }
      
      # Add filtered line to intermediate results
      filtered_results_species <- rbind(filtered_results_species, subset_data)
    }
  }
}

# Convert to data.frame
filtered_results_species <- as.data.frame(filtered_results_species)


# ----------------- STEP 2 : select best line by sample -----------------

# list for final results (1 line by sample only)
final_filtered_results <- list()

# unique sample treatment
for (sample in unique(filtered_results_species$Sample_ID)) {
  
  # Select corresponding line
  subset_sample <- filtered_results_species[filtered_results_species$Sample_ID == sample, ]
  
  # Verify if line exist
  if (nrow(subset_sample) > 0) {
    
    # verify if query_cover > 90
    if (any(subset_sample$Query_Cover > 90)) {
      subset_sample <- subset_sample[subset_sample$Query_Cover > 90, ]  # keep only line with query_cover > 90
    }
    
    # Find max percentage_identity
    max_identity <- max(subset_sample$Percentage_Identity, na.rm = TRUE)
    subset_sample <- subset_sample[subset_sample$Percentage_Identity == max_identity, ]  # keep the one with the max
    
    # if more than one line, keep the one with the highest Query_Cover
    if (nrow(subset_sample) > 1) {
      max_cover <- max(subset_sample$Query_Cover, na.rm = TRUE)
      subset_sample <- subset_sample[subset_sample$Query_Cover == max_cover, ]  # Keep highest query_cover line
    }
    
    # if more than one line remains, keep the first one
    if (nrow(subset_sample) > 1) {
      subset_sample <- subset_sample[1, , drop = FALSE]  # keep the first one
    }
    
    # add filtered to final results
    final_filtered_results <- rbind(final_filtered_results, subset_sample)
  }
}

# Convert final list into data.frame final
final_filtered_results <- as.data.frame(final_filtered_results)
final_filtered_results <- final_filtered_results[, c(setdiff(names(final_filtered_results), "Species")[1:3], "Species", setdiff(names(final_filtered_results), "Species")[-(1:3)])]# order "Species" col into 4th position

View(final_filtered_results)



###################################################################################################
########################################## Found miss identified pool #############################
###################################################################################################

fichier_excel <- "COI_consensus_sequence_table.xlsx"
COI_consensus_sequence_table <- read_excel(fichier_excel)
View(COI_consensus_sequence_table)


species <- unique(final_filtered_results$Species)
species


species_to_identification <- c( # Create a dictionary of corresponding for "Identification_results"
  "Drosophila malerkotliana" = "Bipectinata complex sp.",
  "Drosophila paulistorum" = "D. paulistorum",
  "Drosophila sturtevanti" = "D. sturtevanti",
  "Zaprionus indianus" = "Z. indianus",
  "Drosophila willistoni" = "D. willistoni",
  "Drosophila melanogaster" = "D. melanogaster",
  "Drosophila ananassae" = "D. ananassae",
  "Drosophila prosaltans" = "Saltans sp.",
  "Drosophila polymorpha" = "D. polymorpha",
  "Scaptodrosophila latifasciaeformis" = "Sc. latifasciaeformis",
  "Scaptomyza nigrita" = "Sc. nigrita",
  "Drosophila tropicalis" = "Willistoni sp.",
  "Drosophila nebulosa" = "Willistoni sp.",
  "Drosophila neocordata" = "Saltans sp.",
  "Drosophila nigrodunni" = "D. nigrodunni",
  "Drosophila equinoxialis" = "Willistoni sp.",
  "Drosophila capricorni" = "Willistoni sp.",
  "Drosophila pseudosordidula" = "D.pseudosordidula"
)


final_filtered_results$Identification_results <- species_to_identification[final_filtered_results$Species] #add "Identification_results" column by mapping on "Species" value

final_filtered_results <- final_filtered_results %>%
  mutate(Identification_results = ifelse(Percentage_Identity < 98, NA, Identification_results))

View(final_filtered_results)

############################### Add pool #########################################

fichier_excel2 <- "pool.xlsx"
pool_table <- read_excel(fichier_excel2)
View(pool_table)

# transform pool_table
pool_table_long <- pool_table %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = everything(), names_to = "Pool", values_to = "Sample") %>%
  filter(!is.na(Sample))  # delete empty line to homogenize

# Add Species and Locality col at pool_table_long
n_rows <- nrow(pool_table) # extract information on species et locality from 3 last lines from pool_table
species_info <- pool_table[n_rows - 2, ]
locality_info <- pool_table[n_rows - 1, ]

species_df <- data.frame(Pool = names(species_info), Species = as.character(species_info)) # create dataframes species et locality
locality_df <- data.frame(Pool = names(locality_info), Locality = as.character(locality_info))

pool_table_long <- pool_table_long %>% ## adding species and locality informations with pool_table_long
  left_join(species_df, by = "Pool") %>%
  left_join(locality_df, by = "Pool")
View(pool_table_long)


# merge both tables
merged_result <- final_filtered_results %>%
  left_join(pool_table_long, by = c("Sample_ID" = "Sample"))

merged_result <- merged_result %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    Species = Species.x,
    pool_identification = Species.y
  )
View(merged_result)

write_xlsx(merged_result, "Blast_results_filtered.xlsx")

################################ Isolate potential contamination in pool ###################################
Potential_contamination <- merged_result %>%
  filter(is.na(Identification_results) | Identification_results != pool_identification)
Potential_contamination <- Potential_contamination %>%
  filter(!(Identification_results == "D. willistoni" & pool_identification == "Willistoni sp.") &
         !(Identification_results == "D. melanogaster" & pool_identification == "melanogaster sp.") &
         !(Identification_results == "D. sturtevanti" & pool_identification == "Saltans sp."))
View(Potential_contamination)

write_xlsx(Potential_contamination, "Miss_identify_sample.xlsx")


## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
